Note: the OFL 'hard legal requirement' framing is overstated — OFL requires the license to ship with the font files, not an in-UI credit — but the genre-convention/authorship-credit gap and the undefined surface stand.
confirmedFindings.question: Should the game ship an in-product Credits/About panel (e.g. a subsection of the existing Settings screen) that carries authorship credit, the already-planned version/commit-SHA stamp, and font/asset attributions + license links? Options: (a) define a Settings > About/Credits subsection in §6.9 (and place its reveal — e.g. available from first Settings unlock), carrying author + provenance stamp + OFL/asset notices; (b) keep attribution out of the UI and instead ship license/NOTICE files alongside the bundle (satisfies OFL minimally, no UX work); (c) defer entirely until an asset that legally compels in-product credit (e.g. a CC-BY asset) is actually bundled. confirmedFindings.title: Two named inspiration repos in README — no recorded license-compatibility check before reusing any of their structure/code
confirmedFindings.evidence: README.md lines 19-23 name and link two source projects: 'Inspiration #1: https://github.com/23html/23html.github.io' and 'Inspiration #2: https://github.com/miktaew/yet-another-idle-rpg', and CLAUDE.md/PRD repeatedly reference 'the inspiration games' as a design reference. confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Verified: README.md lines 19-23 name/link two inspiration repos (23html/23html.github.io, miktaew/yet-another-idle-rpg); no LICENSE file exists anywhere in the repo; and the §7.3/M7 pre-publish deploy checklist (docs/prd.md ~4743-4845) contains no license-compatibility, attribution, or credits item (grep returned NONE). confirmedFindings.question: Before the M7 itch.io publish (a public, pay-what-you-want, outward-facing release), do you want a pre-release legal-hygiene checklist item that (a) records the license of each inspiration repo (23html/23html.github.io and miktaew/yet-another-idle-rpg) and (b) confirms the shipped bundle contains no literal copied code/assets from them? confirmedFindings.title: World-sim content (SeasonRules / Festival / WeatherHazard) has typed shapes in §2.14 but no registry module in the §6.5 catalog
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §2.14(c) lines 1351-1354; vs §6.5 registry catalog lines 4104-4121; M4 lines 4627 & 4644
confirmedFindings.evidence: §2.14(c) declares three world-sim data shapes — `SeasonRules { perSeason: { activeNodes[], yieldModifiers, weatherWeights } }`, `Festival { id, season/day, effects (vendorRestock|socialBeat|reputationOpportunity), revealPredicate }`, `WeatherHazard { kind, rateModifiers }`. The §6.5 'one module per type' catalog (lines 4104-4121) lists resources/activities/producers/skills/items/recipes/enemies/areas/dialogues/quests/scrolls/surfaces/ranks/influence/effects/balance — but NO season/festival/weather module. M4 DoD requires building 'the festival/seasonal social layer deepening (§2.14)' (lines 4627, 4644) yet there is nowhere to author this content. (Festivals are specced as MECHANICAL — effects carry vendorRestock/reputationOpportunity — not cosmetic, so they need real data.)
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: §6.5 is the canonical single-source-of-truth registry list and the verifier/gen-docs operate over it. Festivals and weather hazards are mechanical (they restock vendors, grant reputation opportunities, modify labour/combat rates) but have no home module, so M4 cannot author them as data-as-code and the content verifier cannot cross-check their ids. This is the festival counterpart to the known weather-numbers gap, but it is structural (missing registry), not just missing numbers, and parallels — but is distinct from — the known missing belief-beast registry.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Add a world-sim registry (e.g. `content/world.ts` or split `content/festivals.ts` + `content/seasons.ts`) to the §6.5 table to hold SeasonRules/Festival/WeatherHazard, keyed by FestivalId/etc., consistent with the existing one-module-per-type pattern.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed at source. §2.14(c) (prd.md lines 1351-1354) declares three world-sim data shapes — SeasonRules, Festival{id, season/day, effects(vendorRestock|socialBeat|reputationOpportunity), revealPredicate}, WeatherHazard{kind, rateModifiers} — verbatim as claimed. The §6.5 'one module per type' registry catalog (lines 4104-4121) lists 18 modules with none covering seasons/festivals/weather, and the §2.2 Scheduler that §2.14 'reuses' lives in GameState (§6.4), not the content catalog. M4 (lines 4627, 4644) mandates building the festival/seasonal social layer, yet M4 phase-1 'T1 data foundation: registries' (line 4639) enumerates ranks/areas/mobs/coin/surfaces/builds/tunables with no festival/season/weather module — so there is nowhere to author it as data-as-code. Festivals are mechanical (effects carry vendorRestock/reputationOpportunity and a revealPredicate that the §6.6 verifier should cross-check), so a keyed registry is the correct home per the PRD's own 'one module per type' principle. Partial refutation: seasonal yieldModifiers/weather rateModifiers could plausibly sit in balance.ts and season-node gating is already in activities.ts via seasonWindow — but Festival-as-keyed-entity has no covering module, so the structural gap holds. Distinct from the known belief-beast-registry and weather-numbers gaps (different content types). Genuine NEW structural omission in the canonical registry catalog. Not M0/M1-blocking (M4 content) → medium, and the fix requires a design decision (combined vs split module, key-type naming) → needs-human, not clerical.
confirmedFindings.question: §6.5's registry catalog has no home for the §2.14(c) world-sim content shapes (SeasonRules, Festival, WeatherHazard), yet M4 (lines 4627/4644) must author the festival/seasonal social layer as data and Festival is mechanical (effects: vendorRestock|reputationOpportunity; revealPredicate) so the §6.6 verifier needs to cross-check its ids. Where should this content be authored, and how should §6.5 reflect it? Options: (a) one combined content/world.ts keyed by FestivalId/SeasonId/WeatherId; (b) split content/festivals.ts + content/seasons.ts (+ weather folded into balance.ts/effects.ts); (c) treat seasons/weather as balance.ts tunables + activities.ts seasonWindow (already partially covered) and add only a content/festivals.ts row. Whichever is chosen, add the corresponding row(s) to the §6.5 table and reference them in M4 phase-1's registry list so the content verifier can resolve festival effect/reveal references.

confirmedFindings.title: Genuinely un-owned after 4 rounds, but BUILD-gated not doc-gated: sustained-runtime perf/memory profile, the numeric balance.ts simulation, and build correctness
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md:4480-4485 (M0 DoD), 4805-4815 (§7.4 risk register R1-R5), 2204 (§4.0 numbers deferred)
confirmedFindings.evidence: Honest gap-naming per my mandate. Three of the brief's five candidate un-owned areas are REAL but cannot be audited on the doc: (1) Performance/memory at scale — every perf gate in the spec is a one-time LOAD gate ('a fresh game loads in **< 5 s to first interactable**', prd.md:4480; smoke test 'load < 5 s to first interactable', 4796); there is NO sustained-runtime budget (tick cost, DOM-node count, allocation/GC) and the R1-R5 risk register (4811-4815) has no perf/memory row. The event log is a 'capped ring buffer' (2.1, prd.md:899) and the loop is active-only with 'no offline catch-up' (4494), so a literal 28.5h single session is largely mitigated by design — but the actual per-tick cost and steady-state heap are unknown until profiled. (2) The numeric balance.ts simulation is deferred to code that does not exist (§4.0/prd.md:2204; §6.4 'every number lives in balance.ts'); a spec that defers to code cannot be balance-audited on paper. (3) Build correctness — there is no src/ yet. All three only yield signal from running the M0/M1 build.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: These are the legitimate residual blind spots, so the consolidation should record them as 'resolve by building/profiling', not leave them implying more doc audit is owed. Conversely, no round of doc review can close them, which is itself evidence the battery should stop.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Record these three as explicit 'BUILD-to-learn' items, not doc-audit debt: add a lightweight sustained-runtime acceptance line to the M0 (or M1) DoD and an R6 perf/memory row to §7.4.1 (e.g. 'tick + render budget under N ms; steady-state heap bounded across a long session; profiled via the existing chrome-devtools/capture-game-states harness'); treat balance.ts and build correctness as resolved by the first playtest per D-021. Mark provisional — none of this blocks STARTING M0.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason:
Verified against docs/prd.md: every perf gate is a one-time LOAD gate ('< 5 s to first interactable' at the M0 DoD ~4490, §4.8 line 2945, and the §7.3 itch.io smoke test), and the §7.4.1 risk register (R1-R5, ~4811-4815) has no perf/memory row. A grep for heap/GC/tick-cost/allocation/sustained/steady-state/render-budget returns nothing on runtime perf (all 'memory' hits are the narrative amnesia dream-rule). Design-level mitigations the finding credits do exist (capped ring buffer §2.1; active-only loop + no offline catch-up, M0 phase 6), but there is genuinely no quantitative sustained-runtime acceptance criterion nor a perf/memory risk row. Novelty: the rounds-1-3 battery review proposed an 'R6' row only for FUN ('the grind reads as a slog', Q4) and separately covered dtTicks frame-rate determinism (save-determinism-6); neither raised sustained-runtime perf/memory at scale, so this framing is NEW. The finding is also honestly scoped: it is a BUILD-to-learn residual blind spot that does NOT block starting M0/M1 (profilable via the existing chrome-devtools/capture-game-states harness), making it a provisional §7 item — needs-human per D-021, not critical. (Minor: the suggested 'R6 perf' label collides with the already-proposed R6 fun row.)</verdict_reason>
<parameter name="refined_question">The spec's only performance gates are load-time ('< 5 s to first interactable'); there is no sustained-runtime budget (per-tick + render cost, DOM-node count, steady-state heap/GC) and no perf/memory row in the §7.4.1 risk register (R1-R5) — even though v1 targets a ~28.5h single session. The big risks are already mitigated by design (active-only loop, no offline catch-up, capped ring-buffer log), so the residual is only knowable by profiling. Which do you want before M0: (A) add a lightweight sustained-runtime acceptance line to the M0/M1 DoD (e.g. 'tick+render under N ms; steady-state heap bounded across a long session, profiled via the existing chrome-devtools/capture-game-states harness') plus a new perf/memory risk row in §7.4 (note: rename to R7 since R6 is already proposed for the fun risk); or (B) treat perf/memory purely as BUILD-to-learn — keep the load<5s gate as the only committed perf criterion and let the first profiling pass during M0/M1 surface any real budget need? Either way, this does not block STARTING M0.</refined_question>
</invoke>
confirmedFindings.question: Do you want a minimal sustained-runtime perf/memory acceptance criterion (tick/render ms budget + bounded steady-state heap) and an R6 perf risk row added to §7.4 before M0, or is the existing load<5s gate sufficient until profiling during the build surfaces a real problem?

confirmedFindings.title: NEW trivial: LogMessage type field `tICK` is a casing typo for `tick` (saturation signal — round 4 is now surfacing char-level errors)
confirmedFindings.evidence: §2.1: `LogMessage { id, text, channel ('narration'|'reward'|'combat'|'system'), colorClass, tICK }`. The field name `tICK` is a unique (grep count = 1) casing typo for the tick timestamp; every other use in the PRD is lowercase `tick`. Genuinely new (not among the 136). That round 4's targeted lens is now turning up single-token casing typos in type sketches — rather than structural intent or balance defects — is itself corroborating evidence that the doc-level audit has saturated.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: Clerical: a copy-paste of this type into M0's core/log would compile to a wrong field name; cheap to fix now. More importantly it is a marker of diminishing returns.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Rename the field to `tick` to match every other reference in the PRD.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed at docs/prd.md:898. `tICK` appears exactly once in the PRD (grep count = 1) inside the LogMessage type sketch; every other tick reference is lowercase `tick` (line 927 WorldClock { tick, ... }, line 4060 clock: { tick: number }). A LogMessage timestamp field should be `tick`, so this is a clear casing typo — clerical, no judgement needed, and would copy-paste into M0 core/log as a wrong field name. Genuinely a unique single-token error.

clusters.summary: Two clerical inconsistencies sit on the very first thing M0 builds (GameState + its stored surface). (1) This session ADDED currentArea to §6.4 and rewrote the §6.8 persist enumeration in the same pass but never added 'current area' to that enumeration — so the authoritative serialization list the M0 save spine builds against would silently drop player location on save/load, contradicting §6.4/§2.19. (2) The M0 phase-2 surface list names a 'vitals' field that the canonical §6.4 GameState does not have (hp/satiety/attributePoints live under 'character'). Both are safe clerical edits and should be applied before/at the start of M0.

clusters.theme: Save robustness & data durability on a backend-free static host
clusters.summary: The single continuously-overwriting autosave is the only copy of up to 28.5h of no-reset progress, and its robustness story has systematic holes — all on the LOAD side, none on WRITE or durability. (a) Write failures (QuotaExceeded, transaction abort, torn beforeunload commit) have zero handling and no atomicity guarantee — silent data loss. (b) The forward-only migration chain no-ops on a FUTURE-version save (CDN-stale/PWA/re-imported newer backup), risking a silent downgrade that violates the locked 'never wipe Influence/holdings' invariant — this one is a safe-default hardening (trivial-apply). (c) The pre-migration raw-backup lifecycle (atomicity, where it lives vs the single autosave slot, retention, restore trigger, post-chain version assertion) is entirely unspecified. (d) No game-identity tag / import-time registry referential-integrity check. (e) itch serves the game in a cross-origin iframe where IndexedDB is partitioned/ITP-evicted; the M7 smoke is same-session, Chromium-only, never run in-embed or on WebKit/mobile, so it can certify 'save works' while most players lose progress. (f) The base64 export — the documented ONLY backstop — is never placed on a revealed surface and the player is never prompted to back up.

Six distinct a11y gaps in a text-first UI whose labels ARE the information architecture. Trivial-apply: (1) Japanese-script runs are never lang-tagged (WCAG 3.1.2) so kanji pillar/rank/season labels read as glyph names or are skipped; (7) no skip-link and no NAMED landmarks, so keyboard/SR users tab the header vitals + the growing ~9-entry nav before the hero log on every screen; (8) LogMessage.channel enum (4 values) omits the 'milestone/reveal/rank-up' tier that ui-design §5.1 defines as a 5th channel — the one line that must be announced assertively (this edit also fixes the tICK->tick typo, subsuming convergence-critic-4); (6) the M7 keyboard/SR operability gate covers only cold-open + one rung, never the combat panel / four-bar Influence / modals / map. Needs-human: (2) --ink-faint #7A6C59 fails AA contrast (4.23/3.66/3.31:1) on all three paper surfaces yet carries locked-rung hints + disabled-reason text (+ --beni text ~4.0:1, --ai-soft meter 2.44:1) — the palette is locked under D-018 so it's a taste call; (10) no confirm/undo on irreversible actions given the single autosave removes the universal 'reload last save' recovery.

A cluster of pre-publish (M7) legal/hygiene gaps, none blocking M0/M1. (1) No LICENSE file / declared license at all (defaults to all-rights-reserved) on a PWYW public release. (2/3/4) Fonts: licenses never recorded (all are SIL OFL); the recommended self-hosted Option B redistributes the .woff2 with no OFL.txt/copyright bundled (OFL breach) and keeps the Reserved Font Name on a subset (OFL §3 breach); the still-open Option A (Google dynamic subsetting) directly violates the LOCKED zero-network-calls criterion and adds GDPR IP-disclosure exposure. (5) No itch content rating/warnings despite child-spiriting, drowning, grief, debt, violence themes. (6) The half-referenced 'Settings/About' surface is undefined and absent from the reveal ladder — nothing owns in-product credits/attribution. (7) Two named inspiration repos with no recorded license-compatibility / no-copy check. (deploy-host-matrix-4) Audio re-introduces the exact 'asset pipeline' that R4 claims absent to justify its low-risk art rating — no licensing/credits surface, no bundle/lazy-load budget against <5s, no autoplay-gesture gating, zero M7 audio acceptance criteria.

clusters.theme: M1 balance gap — satietyMax has no formula
clusters.summary: satietyMax appears only as a 'derived cap' in the Vitals struct and §2.7 promises satiety capacity scales with level, but no formula exists anywhere in §4 (whereas its sibling hpMax does: 40 + 8·characterLevel + 2·STR + gearHp). M1 phase 3 builds the satiety bar / soft-stamina throttle, which needs a committed cap to render, compute the throttle fraction, and write the test. Needs a committed value (scaling formula vs flat M1 stub) before M1.

clusters.theme: World-sim content has no registry home (M4)
clusters.summary: §2.14(c) declares typed world-sim shapes (SeasonRules, Festival{effects: vendorRestock|reputationOpportunity, revealPredicate}, WeatherHazard) and M4 must author the festival/seasonal social layer as data, but the §6.5 one-module-per-type registry catalog has no season/festival/weather module — so M4 cannot author it as data-as-code and the §6.6 verifier cannot cross-check its ids. Structural gap (missing registry), distinct from the known weather-numbers and belief-beast-registry gaps. Not M0/M1-blocking.

convergence-critic-2: three genuinely un-owned areas remain after 4 rounds but are BUILD-gated, not doc-gated — sustained-runtime perf/memory (every perf gate is a one-time LOAD gate; no per-tick/render budget, no steady-state heap bound, no perf/memory row in §7.4.1 R1-R5), the numeric balance.ts simulation (deferred to code that doesn't exist), and build correctness (no src/ yet). All only yield signal from running M0/M1; record as build-to-learn, not doc-audit debt. convergence-critic-4: the tICK casing typo at line 898 — that round 4 is surfacing single-token typos rather than structural defects is itself the saturation signal (this typo is folded into the a11y-text-ui-deep-8 edit).

trivial_fixes.old_string: RNG (seed + counter), clock, resources, producer counts
trivial_fixes.new_string: RNG (seed + counter), clock, current area, resources, producer counts
trivial_fixes.rationale: This session added currentArea to §6.4 (persisted, non-derivable) and rewrote the §6.8 persist enumeration in the same pass, but omitted 'current area' from that enumeration. A literal §6.8 serializer would drop player location on save/load, contradicting §6.4/§2.19. Insert 'current area' after 'clock' to match §6.4 field ordering. Unique substring.

trivial_fixes.old_string: (M0 surface: schemaVersion, rng, clock, vitals, resources, flags, unlocked, log)
trivial_fixes.new_string: (M0 surface: schemaVersion, rng, clock, character {hp, satiety, attributePoints} — the §6.4 stored field; there is no separate top-level `vitals`, resources, flags, unlocked, log)
trivial_fixes.rationale: The M0 phase-2 surface list names a 'vitals' field that the canonical §6.4 GameState lacks (hp/satiety/attributePoints live under 'character'). Pointing the M0 line at the real §6.4 field prevents the M0 coder inventing a 'vitals' slice that must be reconciled later. The first type M0 builds.

trivial_fixes.new_string: function, unit-tested). Migrations live in `persistence/migrations/`. **A save whose `schemaVersion` exceeds the running build's** (a future-version save opened by an older / CDN-stale build — there is no forward step to run) is treated as **incompatible** and takes the same calm degrade-gracefully recovery as a corrupt save, with the raw bytes preserved for re-import in a newer build — never a silent half-load and never a downgrade that overwrites the real autosave.
trivial_fixes.rationale: Forward-only migration chain no-ops on a future-version save (CDN-stale/PWA/re-imported newer backup), risking a silent downgrade that violates the locked 'never wipe Influence/holdings' invariant. Safe-default hardening: extend the existing recovery path to a version ceiling. (Upstream new_string carried trailing </verified_new></invoke> XML — stripped.)

trivial_fixes.old_string:
- **Semantic structure + live region:** the event/story log is an ARIA live region so reveals/important events
  are announced to assistive tech; screens/panels use semantic landmarks and labelled controls.
trivial_fixes.new_string:

Both are standard a11y practice with no design trade-off.

Adds the 5th 'milestone' channel that ui-design §5.1's table defines (so per-channel ARIA politeness can route reveals as assertive) AND fixes the unique tICK->tick casing typo. This single edit subsumes convergence-critic-4 (which only fixed tICK); do not apply both.

trivial_fixes.old_string: verified by a keyboard-only and a touch-only run of the cold open + one rung.
trivial_fixes.new_string: verified by a keyboard-only and a touch-only run of the cold open + one rung AND of a late state (force-loaded via `window.__qa`) exercising the combat panel (stance/ability/item/retreat), the four-bar Influence panel, a focus-trapped modal, and the map screen — so operability is proven on the dense revealed UI, not only the single-column open.
trivial_fixes.rationale: The keyboard/SR operability gate covers only cold-open + one rung; the hard surfaces (combat verbs, four-bar Influence, modals, map) are never operability-verified, so the gate can pass with the complex UI unchecked. Reuses already-listed signature states via __qa. Unique to line 4719 (line 4708 reads 'a rung', not 'one rung').

human_questions.question: What storage-durability posture does v1 commit to for itch.io's cross-origin iframe embed, and should it become M7 verify-gate acceptance criteria? The single autosave is the only copy of up to 28.5h of no-reset progress, living in third-party partitioned IndexedDB (Chrome/Firefox partition, WebKit ITP eviction) that the current same-session, Chromium-only, raw-URL smoke test cannot detect; and the base64 export — the documented ONLY backstop — is never placed on a revealed surface nor proactively prompted.
human_questions.context: Provisional §7/M7 plan item (D-021). deploy-host-matrix-1 + deploy-host-matrix-2. §6.8 locks a single autosave with no write-probe/fallback. The export's home depends on accessibility-ux-1 (Settings missing from the §3.5 reveal ladder).
options: (a) Accept ephemeral third-party storage and lean on a proactively-prompted base64 export as the real save (cheapest; export already named as backstop but never surfaced/prompted)

(b) Add a boot-time write-probe that detects unavailable/partitioned IndexedDB and surfaces a calm 'export a backup' banner, place export/import on a surface revealed by ~end of T0, add a one-time backup nudge (e.g. after first tier-up), AND require the M7 smoke to run INSIDE the actual itch embed on Chromium AND WebKit/Safari (+ mobile-in-frame) with save-survives-reload-in-embed
(c) Something stronger (e.g. redundant localStorage mirror)
human_questions.recommendation: (b) — it is the only path that actually catches silent autosave loss for most players and fixes the unsurfaced-backstop gap, at the cost of a WebKit/embed QA matrix the Chromium-only tooling does not cover plus a new §6.8 detection/fallback code path.

human_questions.question: How should a FAILED autosave WRITE be handled? §6.8/§2.19 robustness language is entirely LOAD-side; a rejected IndexedDB put (QuotaExceeded, transaction abort, private-mode unavailability) or a torn beforeunload commit has no spec, and nothing guarantees the write is atomic.
human_questions.context: §6 tech-spec gap, does not block M0 but informs the M0 save spine (phase 5). save-write-failure-quota-1. Silent write failure is worse than a load error — the player keeps playing believing they are saved, then loses everything on reload.
options: (A) Specify autosave as a single atomic put in ONE transaction (never clear-then-write) AND surface a calm persistent 'couldn't save — export a backup now' notice on any write rejection
(B) Atomic put + silent retry only, no player-facing notice (simpler, risks silent loss)
(C) Atomic put + on FIRST failure auto-prompt the base64 export flow (loudest/safest, most intrusive)
human_questions.recommendation: (A) — atomicity guarantee is non-negotiable on a single-slot autosave; the calm notice matches the existing 'never a scary wall' ethos without the intrusiveness of (C). Confirm notice loudness.

human_questions.question: Define the migration / pre-migration-backup lifecycle for §6.8. The spec says only 'a backup of the raw bytes is kept' — leaving atomicity, backup location, retention, restore trigger, and a post-chain version assertion all undefined.
human_questions.context: §6 tech-spec gap (D-021), bounded, does not block M0. save-migration-atomicity-backup-1. The debounced autosave fires on a tick interval, so a backup sharing the single autosave slot would be clobbered; a per-step write-back corrupts the very save the backup protects.
options: (a) Commit model: run the whole chain in-memory and write back ONCE atomically on success vs write-back per step
(b) Backup location: a SEPARATE IndexedDB key the autosave never touches vs reuse the autosave slot
(c) Retention: keep until next clean load / N saves vs delete on success
(d) Restore trigger: auto-rollback + recovery UI on a thrown migration vs manual export only (note the current recovery UI never references the backup)
(e) Add a post-chain assert that final schemaVersion === build version
human_questions.recommendation: In-memory single atomic commit; raw bytes under a separate never-autosaved key; auto-rollback with the calm recovery UI; add the post-chain version assertion.

human_questions.question: Should the save import/load path add (1) a constant game-identity/magic field (e.g. app:"kami-kakushi") that import must match, and (2) a referential-integrity pass over stored ids (ItemId/QuestId/ProducerId/equipDefId/SurfaceId) against the live registries?
human_questions.context: §6 tech policy (D-021), low severity. save-game-identity-integrity-1. Mainly bites hand-edited/foreign imports and unversioned content edits, since normal content changes ship with a unit-tested migration; the §6.6 verifier runs at test time only.
options: (a) Reject the whole save into the existing calm-recovery flow on any unknown id (safest for integrity)
(b) Drop/default unknown ids and continue with a logged warning (preserves more progress, risks silently losing items/quests)
(c) No change (rely on structural validate + migration discipline)
human_questions.recommendation: Add the magic field (cheap fast-reject); for stale ids prefer (a) reject-to-recovery — silent item/quest loss is worse than a recovery prompt for a no-reset game.

human_questions.question: satietyMax has no formula while its sibling hpMax does (40 + 8·characterLevel + 2·STR + gearHp), yet §2.7 promises satiety capacity grows with level. Pick a committed value for M1.
human_questions.context: Provisional §4 balance item (D-021); a true M1 blocker — M1 phase 3 builds the satiety bar / soft-stamina throttle and needs a cap to render, compute the throttle fraction, and write the test. m0m1-blocker-triage-2.
options: (A) Commit a scaling formula in §4 parallel to hpMax, e.g. satietyMax = SATIETY_BASE + perLevel·characterLevel + attrTerm (keeps §2.7's leveling reward real; adds a constant to tune at M6)
(B) Commit a fixed M1 stub (e.g. satietyMax = 100) and defer level/attribute scaling (fastest to build/test; temporarily makes §2.7 inert)
human_questions.recommendation: (B) a flat stub for M1 to unblock the build, with a noted intent to promote to the (A) scaling formula by M6 — consistent with §4's provisional-numbers philosophy.

human_questions.question: --ink-faint #7A6C59 fails WCAG AA (4.5:1) on all three paper surfaces (4.23/3.66/3.31:1) yet carries load-bearing affordance text — locked-rung unlock hints (§5.4), the disabled-button 'why' reason (§5.5), captions/system-log. (Also --beni text ~4.0:1 on shade/deep cards, and the --ai-soft meter fill vs --washi-deep track 2.44:1, below the 3:1 non-text floor.) The D-018 palette is locked intent, so this is a taste/design call.
human_questions.context: ui-design.md locked palette (D-018). a11y-text-ui-deep-2. Does not block M0/M1.
options: (a) Darken --ink-faint toward ~#6A5A45 (~4.5:1 on --washi only, so also restrict hint/reason text to the --washi base surface)
(b) Keep --ink-faint as decorative/muted-label only; render all locked-hint + disabled-reason text in --ink-soft #4A3F33 (8.49:1, passes everywhere)
(c) Accept a documented sub-AA tradeoff for muted captions only, with an explicit note that hint/reason text must not use it
human_questions.recommendation: (b) — move functional hint/reason text to --ink-soft (passes on every surface) and keep --ink-faint purely decorative; separately switch the meter fill to --ai #27496D (or a darker track) to clear the 3:1 non-text floor.

human_questions.question: Should irreversible/destructive in-game actions (material-consuming craft, sell, committed attribute-point allocation — §4.4 no-respec is LOCKED, and later T3 narrative-route commitments) require a confirm step or a brief undo window, given the single continuously-overwriting autosave provides no 'reload last save' recovery?
human_questions.context: §2.19/§6.11 UX policy, low severity, does not block M0/M1. a11y-text-ui-deep-10. WCAG 2.2 3.3.4/3.3.6. The 'dents not wipes' philosophy softens economic stalls but not an in-the-moment destructive misclick.
options: (a) Confirm only on high-cost/rare actions (rare-material consume, sell equipped/last-of-kind, attribute-point allocation); leave routine fast-loop verbs unguarded
(b) A short undo window on the last destructive intent instead of a modal (less friction in the incremental loop)
(c) Status quo — rely on 'dents not wipes' and accept WCAG 3.3.4/3.3.6 non-conformance for game actions
human_questions.recommendation: (a) — guard only the genuinely unrecoverable, rare actions (allocation, narrative-route, rare-material consume) and record the action-class policy in §2.19/§6.11; keeps the fast loop frictionless.

human_questions.question: Audio (§2.21(a)/§6.9/M6) is the only v1 element outside the 'text + emoji + CSS' register, yet R4 rates art-risk low on the premise of 'no asset pipeline' and M7 ships with zero audio acceptance criteria (no licensing/credits surface, no bundle/lazy-load budget against the <5s load, no autoplay-gesture gating). Park audio, or scope it as a real pipeline?
human_questions.context: Provisional §7/M7 plan item (D-021); also corrects an internal inconsistency in §7.4.1 R4. deploy-host-matrix-4. Unlicensed audio is a takedown exposure on a public PWYW page.
options: (A) Park audio from v1 so R4's 'no asset pipeline / low-risk' posture is literally true

(B) Treat audio as a scoped pipeline: every clip permissively licensed + attributed in an in-game Credits/About surface, an audio bundle/lazy-load budget that keeps <5s load, and audio that starts only after the first user gesture (the 'Open your eyes' click) respecting mute + prefers-reduced-motion — plus M7 audio acceptance criteria
(C) Specify audio as synthesized (Web Audio, no files → no licensing/bundle exposure)
human_questions.recommendation: (C) synthesized Web Audio if the feel allows (kills the licensing/bundle/credits exposure outright); else (B). Either way correct R4 to acknowledge audio as the one asset outside text+emoji+CSS.

human_questions.question: Before any public release / itch.io upload (M7), what license(s) does the project adopt? There is no LICENSE file and no declared license anywhere, so the default is all-rights-reserved on a PWYW public-style repo.
human_questions.context: Legal, low severity, M7-only (not M0/M1 blocking). legal-attribution-1. Affects what the PWYW community may do, whether the repo can be public, and what notice the itch page must carry.
options: (a) CODE: permissive (MIT/Apache-2.0) vs source-available vs all-rights-reserved
(b) NARRATIVE/CONTENT & balance data: CC-BY / CC0 vs proprietary
(c) Is the repo intended to be public?
human_questions.recommendation: Decide before M7; a split of permissive code (MIT/Apache-2.0) + proprietary-or-CC-BY content is the common fit for a 'built agentically, inspired by two OSS games' ethos. Once decided the fix is clerical (add LICENSE + README note + itch page reference).

human_questions.question: Lock the font delivery + licensing approach. The DRAFT stack is all SIL OFL Google Fonts; (a) Option A (Google dynamic subsetting) violates the LOCKED zero-network-calls criterion (§7.3) and adds GDPR IP-disclosure; (b) the recommended self-hosted Option B redistributes the .woff2 with no OFL.txt/copyright bundled (OFL breach) and (c) keeps the Reserved Font Name on a subset (OFL §3 breach).
human_questions.context: Provisional §7/M7 + ui-design.md §3.2 (font deferred to M1, flagged for human). legal-attribution-2 + -3 + -4. Not M0/M1 blocking but the first itch zip is non-compliant without it.
options: Eliminate Option A — mark it INCOMPATIBLE with the zero-network criterion in §3.2 / open-choice #2 and lock self-hosted Option B (accepting the one-time subset build step)
Rename the self-hosted subset's internal name table during the pyftsubset build (e.g. 'Kurosawa Mincho Subset') to satisfy the OFL RFN rule, and reference that name in CSS
Add to build:itch / M7 a step copying each font's OFL text + copyright header into dist/ (e.g. /fonts/LICENSE-OFL.txt)
human_questions.recommendation: Lock Option B (kill Option A as network/GDPR-incompatible), rename the subset name table to clear the RFN rule, and add the OFL-bundling deploy step + a one-line 'all families OFL' note in §3.1. All three together.

human_questions.question: What content rating and content-warning descriptors should the itch page declare, given themes of child-disappearance (kamikakushi), drowning/death imagery, grief, debt, and on-screen combat?
human_questions.context: Provisional §7/M7 deploy-runbook item (D-021), low severity, human classification call. legal-attribution-5. itch does not mandate ratings for mild folklore/violence, but the uploader is prompted for them.
options: (A) Leave unrated (lowest effort; minor expectations-mismatch risk)
(B) Add a content warning/descriptor (e.g. 'mild fantasy violence; themes of death, child-disappearance and grief') + a one-line page note, adult-content toggle OFF
(C) (B) plus explicit age guidance
human_questions.recommendation: (B) — near-zero cost, improves expectation-setting; add as a deploy-checklist step in §7.3/M7.

human_questions.question: Should the game define an in-product Credits/About surface (carrying authorship, the planned commit-SHA stamp, and font/asset attributions + license links), and add a pre-release check of the two named inspiration repos' licenses / no-copy confirmation?
human_questions.context: §6.9 + legal, low severity, M7-ish. legal-attribution-6 + legal-attribution-7. The 'Settings/About' surface is alluded to (commit-SHA stamp only) but never defined or placed on the reveal ladder; README cites two inspiration repos with no recorded license check.
options: (a) Define a Settings > About/Credits subsection in §6.9 (author + provenance stamp + OFL/asset notices) and add a one-line attestation that the build is a clean-room rebuild with zero copied code/assets
(b) Keep attribution out of the UI and ship license/NOTICE files alongside the bundle (satisfies OFL minimally)
(c) Defer until an asset legally compels in-product credit
human_questions.recommendation: (a) — define the About/Credits subsection (small scope, pre-satisfies attribution + gives the build stamp a home) and record the clean-room attestation; it also resolves the half-referenced surface.

human_questions.question: Where should the §2.14(c) world-sim content (SeasonRules, Festival, WeatherHazard) be authored, and how should §6.5 reflect it? The registry catalog currently has no home for these mechanical (vendorRestock/reputationOpportunity + revealPredicate) shapes that M4 must author as data.
human_questions.context: §6.5 registry / M4 content (D-021), medium, not M0/M1 blocking. least-audited-deep-4. The §6.6 verifier needs to cross-check festival effect/reveal ids.
options: (a) One combined content/world.ts keyed by FestivalId/SeasonId/WeatherId
(b) Split content/festivals.ts + content/seasons.ts (+ weather folded into balance.ts/effects.ts)
(c) Seasons/weather as balance.ts tunables + activities.ts seasonWindow (partly covered) and add only a content/festivals.ts row
human_questions.recommendation: (c) — Festival is the only true keyed-entity gap; add a content/festivals.ts row to §6.5 and reference it in M4 phase-1's registry list, leaving season/weather modifiers in balance.ts/activities.ts where they partly live already.

human_questions.question: Do you want a minimal sustained-runtime perf/memory acceptance criterion (per-tick + render ms budget, bounded steady-state heap) plus a new perf/memory risk row in §7.4, or treat perf/memory purely as build-to-learn (keep load<5s as the only committed perf gate until profiling surfaces a real need)?
human_questions.context: Provisional §7 item (D-021); does NOT block STARTING M0. convergence-critic-2. The big risks are already design-mitigated (active-only loop, no offline catch-up, capped ring-buffer log); residual is only knowable by profiling via the existing chrome-devtools/capture-game-states harness. NOTE: the new risk row should be R7, not R6 — R6 is already proposed for the fun-slog risk.
options: (A) Add a lightweight sustained-runtime acceptance line to the M0/M1 DoD + an R7 perf/memory risk row in §7.4
(B) Build-to-learn — keep load<5s as the only committed perf criterion and let the first profiling pass surface any budget need
human_questions.recommendation: (B) for now, with a noted intent to add the R7 row once the first M0/M1 profiling run gives real numbers — avoids inventing a budget before there is code to measure.

synthesis.convergence_verdict: STOP + CONSOLIDATE — the doc-level audit has saturated. Round 4's targeted lenses are now surfacing single-token casing typos (tICK at line 898) and clerical list-omissions rather than structural intent or balance defects, which is the convergence-critic's own stated saturation signal. The remaining genuinely un-owned areas (sustained-runtime perf/memory, the numeric balance.ts simulation, build correctness) are BUILD-gated, not doc-gated — no further round of doc review can close them; they only yield signal from running M0/M1. Do NOT run another full review round. SINGLE HIGHEST-VALUE NEXT ACTION: apply the 6 trivial fixes (especially the fix-reverification-1 regression on the §6.8 persist list and the M0 surface-list type drift), get the ~3 M0 save-spine human decisions (Q44/Q45/Q46) answered alongside the version-ceiling hardening, then START M0 and let the first build/profiling pass resolve the build-to-learn residuals.
cross_cutting_risks: DATA LOSS is the dominant cross-cutting risk and recurs across four independent lenses (save-migration, deploy-host-matrix, a11y, convergence): a no-reset 28.5h-progression game stakes everything on ONE continuously-overwriting autosave, yet write-failure, future-version downgrade, backup lifecycle, import integrity, third-party-iframe partition/eviction, and the unsurfaced base64 backstop are each individually under-specified. Any one can silently destroy the entire pitch (the long climb). These should be resolved as a single coherent persistence-hardening pass, not piecemeal.

The base64 export is simultaneously named as the load-bearing safety net (§7.3) AND left undiscoverable (no revealed surface, no prompt, Settings absent from the §3.5 reveal ladder) — it is the connective tissue between the storage-durability finding, the export-discoverability finding, the write-failure finding, and the Credits/About-surface finding. Defining the Settings/About surface and placing it on the reveal ladder unblocks several findings at once.

The 'no asset pipeline → low-risk' framing (R4) is quietly false in two places: audio (sourced clips, licensing, bundle, autoplay) and self-hosted fonts (OFL redistribution + Reserved Font Name). The PRD's risk posture and M7 acceptance gate both rest on a premise the content actually violates.

M7 acceptance gates systematically under-test the hard cases: same-session/Chromium-only/raw-URL save smoke (misses real durability), keyboard-SR run scoped to cold-open+one-rung (misses the dense UI), zero audio criteria, and load-only perf gates (no sustained runtime). The release gate can go green while the riskiest surfaces are unverified.

Locked decisions (D-018 palette, §4.4 no-respec, §6.8 single autosave, zero-network-calls) collide with newly-surfaced accessibility/durability needs (ink-faint AA contrast, no-undo on irreversible actions, font Option A). Several findings are genuinely human taste/policy calls precisely because the obvious technical fix would breach a locked decision.
